untranslatable_reason: This signal requires options-specific indicators and data that are not available in the allowed indicator set: (1) Strike prices and moneyness calculations for both long and short call legs; (2) Option delta values to assess early exercise probability; (3) Implied volatility and dividend yield data; (4) Assignment probability assessment; (5) Vertical spread width constraints relative to strike differences; (6) Ex-dividend date proximity tracking. The signal is fundamentally an options strategy that cannot be expressed using only price-action and technical indicators on underlying assets.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_118_0 describes a complex dividend-capture vertical spread strategy requiring options chain analysis, Greeks (delta), and dividend/corporate action calendars. The entry conditions involve deeply ITM spreads, moneyness thresholds, dividend yield filters, and assignment risk assessment—none of which can be derived from the allowed technical indicators. This strategy requires an options-aware backtesting engine with dividend data, volatility surface, and assignment probability models.
